evals.prompt: I want to get started with Unsloth. Verify it's set up correctly and show me what commands are available.
expectations: Verifies unsloth CLI is installed (e.g. unsloth --help, which unsloth, or pip show)
Verifies studio venv exists or runs unsloth studio setup
Shows available commands (train, inference, export, list-checkpoints, studio)
Runs a quick sanity check (e.g. unsloth train --dry-run or unsloth list-checkpoints)
No errors in the verification commands

evals.prompt: Install Unsloth from scratch and set up the Studio environment. This is a clean machine with nothing installed.
expectations: Installs unsloth via one-liner (curl -fsSL https://unsloth.ai/install.sh | sh), pip install unsloth, or uv pip install unsloth
Studio setup is included (either via the one-liner or explicit unsloth studio setup)
Install comes before studio setup
Verifies the CLI works after install (e.g. unsloth --help)
No unnecessary exploration before installing
evals.notes: Best run in a clean Docker container. Use: docker run --rm -it ubuntu:24.04 bash

evals.prompt: I need to install Unsloth on a clean Linux server. Use Docker to spin up a clean Ubuntu container and run the one-liner install to verify it works end-to-end. Docker is already running on this machine.
expectations: Runs a Docker container with a clean Ubuntu image (e.g. ubuntu:24.04)
Installs curl if not present (apt-get install curl)
Runs the one-liner: curl -fsSL https://unsloth.ai/install.sh | sh
The install completes successfully (uv, Python 3.13, unsloth, and studio setup all finish)
Verifies the CLI works inside the container (e.g. unsloth --help or unsloth list-checkpoints)
No errors in the install output (torchcodec platform warnings are acceptable)
evals.notes: Requires Docker daemon running. Takes ~2-5 min depending on network. The torchcodec aarch64 warning is expected on ARM and non-fatal. Known issue: on x86_64 Docker without GPU, uv may resolve unsloth==2024.8 instead of the latest version due to --torch-backend=auto (see upstream issue #4488). On ARM (e.g. Apple Silicon Docker) the install works correctly.

evals.prompt: I need to install Unsloth on a fresh Windows machine using PowerShell. Run the one-liner install and set up the Studio environment.
expectations: Uses the PowerShell one-liner: irm https://unsloth.ai/install.ps1 | iex
The install completes successfully (Python, uv, unsloth, and studio setup all finish)
Activates the venv after install: .\unsloth_studio\Scripts\activate
Verifies the CLI works (e.g. unsloth --help)
No unnecessary exploration before installing
evals.notes: This eval requires a Windows environment. It will fail on macOS/Linux. Skip when running on non-Windows platforms.

evals.prompt: Fine-tune Qwen3-0.6B on the Alpaca dataset with LoRA, 3 epochs, then export to GGUF q4_k_m.
expectations: Uses non-GGUF model ID: unsloth/Qwen3-0.6B (not -GGUF variant)
Writes a YAML config or uses correct CLI flags for training
Sets num_epochs to 3
Uses --dry-run before actual training
After training, runs unsloth list-checkpoints to find checkpoint path
Runs export with --format gguf --quantization q4_k_m
No hallucinated flags

evals.prompt: Run inference on Qwen3-0.6B with the prompt 'Summarize the benefits of LoRA' at temperature 0.5.
expectations: Uses correct command: unsloth inference
Model and prompt are positional arguments
Uses --temperature 0.5
No unnecessary flags or exploration

evals.prompt: I'm on a Mac with no NVIDIA GPU. Run inference with a small model and the prompt 'What is LoRA?' — make sure it actually works.
expectations: Uses a GGUF model ID (e.g. unsloth/Llama-3.2-1B-Instruct-GGUF or similar -GGUF variant)
Uses correct command: unsloth inference with model and prompt as positional args
Inference actually succeeds (exit code 0, model response generated)
Does NOT try a non-GGUF model that would fail on Mac

evals.prompt: I have local JSONL files at ./data/train.jsonl. Fine-tune Qwen3-0.6B on them.
expectations: Uses non-GGUF model ID: unsloth/Qwen3-0.6B
Writes a YAML config file (local_dataset is YAML-only, no CLI flag)
Config includes local_dataset with ./data/train.jsonl
Does NOT use --dataset with a local file path (that's the HuggingFace field)
Uses --dry-run before training
Uses config file approach: unsloth train --config <file>
